messages.content: You are a Lean 4 autoformalization agent working in a current Mathlib-only project. You must formalize the provided TeX/math source chunk into one Lean theorem or lemma, including a proof, using only the Lean prefix context provided. The target Lean statement, target declaration name, and original proof are intentionally withheld. Avoid stale Lean 3/old Mathlib syntax and identifiers; follow the lean_environment/current_version_guidance and local Lean examples. Return exactly one JSON object.

{

    "Do not assume access to the withheld target Lean statement or name.",
    "For multi-part TeX chunks, formalize only the specified labeled part/source span. Do not conjoin all parts unless the record explicitly asks for the whole multi-part result.",
    "Do not cite or invent raw helper names that are not present in the Lean prefix context/local examples; prefer displayed local style and standard Mathlib APIs.",
    "Do not redeclare definitions, structures, abbrevs, notation helpers, or instances already present in the Lean prefix context; reference them directly.",
    "Do not introduce theorem-local `where` definitions or redefine concepts such as summability, limits, binomial coefficients, or matrix operations. If a needed definition is not in context, state a narrower theorem using the displayed APIs.",
    "Use current Lean 4/Mathlib syntax and API names; if your memory conflicts with displayed local context, trust the displayed context.",
    "State a single proposition-level theorem/lemma that is likely to match the specified source part; do not bundle typeclass instances or unrelated source parts into conjunctions.",
    "Every nonstandard helper theorem or local API used in the proof should appear explicitly in the Lean prefix context or local examples. If it is not displayed, do not use its name.",
    "If an existing theorem or lemma in the prefix context has binders, apply it to the needed variables rather than using the theorem constant bare.",
    "Prefer the narrowest theorem directly supported by the source sentence; avoid generalizing to a stronger forall/if statement unless that exact shape is in the source or prefix context.",
    "Prefer a short proof if the prefix context already contains the needed fact."
  ],
  "context": {
    "source_statement_or_chunk": [
      {
        "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/tex/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.tex",
        "line_range": [

        ],

        "line_range_adjustment_reasons": [

        "labels": [
          "lem.sf.alternant-0"
        ],
        "snippet": "\\begin{lemma}\n\\label{lem.sf.alternant-0}Let $\\alpha\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(a)} If the $N$-tuple $\\alpha$ has two equal entries, then $a_{\\alpha\n}=0$. \\medskip\n\n\\textbf{(b)} Let $\\beta\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ be an $N$-tuple obtained from\n$\\alpha$ by swapping two entries. Then, $a_{\\beta}=-a_{\\alpha}$.\n\\end{lemma}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}.]This is an easy consequence of\nDefinition \\ref{def.sf.alternants} \\textbf{(b)}. See Section\n\\ref{sec.details.sf.schur} for a detailed proof.\n\\end{proof}\n\n\\begin{proof}\n[Proof of Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.stemb-lem}.]For any $\\beta\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ and\nany $i\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $, we let $\\beta_{i}$ denote the $i$-th entry of\n$\\beta$. Thus, for example, $\\rho_{k}=N-k$ for each $k\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $\n(since $\\rho=\\left(  N-1,N-2,\\ldots,N-N\\right)  $).\n\nSince addition on $\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ is defined entrywise, we have $\\left(\n\\beta+\\gamma\\right)  _{i}=\\beta_{i}+\\gamma_{i}$ for any $\\beta,\\gamma\n\\in\\mathbb{N}^{N}$ and $i\\in\\left[  N\\right]  $.\n\nThe group $S_{N}$ acts on $\\mathcal{P}$ by $K$-algebra automorphisms. Hence,\nin particular, we have%\n\\[\n\\sigma\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  =\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)  \\cdot\\left(\n\\sigma\\cdot g\\right)\n\\]\nfor any $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$ and any $f,g\\in\\mathcal{P}$. In other words, we have%\n\\begin{equation}\n\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot f\\right)  \\cdot\\left(  \\sigma\\cdot g\\right)  =\\sigma\n\\cdot\\left(  fg\\right)  \\label{pf.lem.sf.stemb-lem.sigfg=}%\n\\end{equation}\nfor any $\\sigma\\in S_{N}$ and any $f,g\\in\\mathcal{P}$.\n"
      }
    ],

    "source_progress_context": {
      "prior_same_label_declarations": [
        {
          "declaration": "alternant_zero_of_eq",
          "path": "AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean",
          "line_range": [

          ],

          "doc_excerpt": "An alternant is zero if two entries of α are equal.\nLemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}(a) in the source.\n\nThis delegates to `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated`.\ntheorem alternant_zero_of_eq {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n(i j : Fin N) (hij : i ≠ j) (heq : α i = α j) :\nalternant (R := R) N α = 0 :=\nAlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_",
          "declaration_head": "/-- An alternant is zero if two entries of α are equal.\n    Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}(a) in the source.\n\n    This delegates to `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated`. -/\ntheorem alternant_zero_of_eq {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n    (i j : Fin N) (hij : i ≠ j) (heq : α i = α j) :\n    alternant (R",
          "policy": "prior same-file declaration only; target declaration is not inspected"
        }
      ],
      "instruction": "When the source span has multiple labeled or lettered parts, use prior_same_label_declarations as prefix-progress evidence. If an earlier declaration already formalizes one part, prefer the remaining or next source part instead of bundling all parts.",

    },
    "context_mode": "source-only",
    "available_imports": [
      "Mathlib",
      "AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.LittlewoodRichardson",

      "AlgebraicCombinatorics.Permutations.Basics"
    ],
    "lean_prefix_context": "-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:45-45 (open)\nopen Finset Matrix Polynomial MvPolynomial BigOperators\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:47-47 (variable)\nvariable (N : ℕ) [NeZero N]\n\n-- File context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:139-139 (variable)\nvariable {R : Type*} [CommRing R]\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:141-150\n/-- The alternant a_α for an N-tuple α.\n    Definition \\ref{def.sf.alternants}(b) in the source.\n\n    This is an abbreviation for `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant` from LittlewoodRichardson.lean,\n    which defines it as ∑_{σ ∈ S_N} sign(σ) · x^(σ·α). This equals the determinant-based\n    definition det(x_j^{α_i}) by `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det`.\n\n    The explicit `N` parameter is kept for backward compatibility with existing code. -/\nnoncomputable abbrev alternant (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant α\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:405-406\nnoncomputable abbrev xPow (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.SymmetricFunctions.monomialExp α\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:859-867\n/-- The alternant a_α = ∑_{σ ∈ S_N} sign(σ) · x^(σ·α).\n    Here σ·α means (α_{σ(1)}, α_{σ(2)}, ..., α_{σ(N)}).\n\n    **Equivalence with SchurBasics.alternant:**\n    This sum-based definition equals the determinant-based definition in\n    `alternant` (from SchurBasics.lean). See `alternant_eq_det` for the proof\n    that this equals `det(alternantMatrix α)`. -/\nnoncomputable def alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  ∑ σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin N), Equiv.Perm.sign σ • xPow (α ∘ σ)\n\n-- Predecessor context: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/LittlewoodRichardson.lean:1045-1099\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:152-157 (alternantTextbook)\n/-- Alternative definition matching the textbook convention exactly:\n    entry (i, j) = x_i^{α_j}. This equals `alternant` since det(A) = det(A^T). -/\ndef alternantTextbook (N : ℕ) (α : Fin N → ℕ) : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R :=\n  Matrix.det (Matrix.of fun i j => (X i : MvPolynomial (Fin N) R) ^ α j)\n\nomit [NeZero N] in\n-- Local API retrieval: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:158-167 (alternantTextbook_eq_alternant)\n/-- The two alternant definitions are equal.\n    This uses `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det` to bridge the sum-based\n    alternant definition with the determinant-based alternantTextbook. -/\ntheorem alternantTextbook_eq_alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) :\n    alternantTextbook (R := R) N α = alternant N α := by\n  unfold alternantTextbook alternant\n  rw [AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det]\n  unfold AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternantMatrix\n  rw [← Matrix.det_transpose]\n  congr 1\n-- Same-file source-label API: AlgebraicCombinatorics/SymmetricFunctions/SchurBasics.lean:3073-3082 (alternant_zero_of_eq)\n/-- An alternant is zero if two entries of α are equal.\n    Lemma \\ref{lem.sf.alternant-0}(a) in the source.\n\n    This delegates to `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated`. -/\ntheorem alternant_zero_of_eq {R : Type*} [CommRing R] {α : Fin N → ℕ}\n    (i j : Fin N) (hij : i ≠ j) (heq : α i = α j) :\n    alternant (R := R) N α = 0 :=\n  AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_zero_of_repeated ⟨i, j, hij, heq⟩\n\nomit [NeZero N] in",
    "lean_environment": {
      "toolchain": "leanprover/lean4:v4.28.0",
      "imports": [
        "Mathlib"
      ],
      "current_version_guidance": [
        "Generated code is checked with the repository lean-toolchain and current Mathlib, not Lean 3 or an older Lean 4 snapshot.",
        "Prefer Lean 4 syntax: `fun x => ...`, `by` tactic blocks, namespaces/dot notation as shown in the prompt, and current Mathlib names from displayed context.",
        "Do not invent old/deprecated identifiers such as guessed `*_apply`, `det_swap_rows`, `CommAlgebra`, or `LaurentPolynomial.X` unless they are explicitly present in context; use displayed local APIs or prove by unfolding/simping.",
        "For power-series coefficients, follow the local examples' argument order such as `coeff n (X : R⟦X⟧)`, not `coeff (X : R⟦X⟧) n`.",
        "For Laurent polynomial samples, prefer displayed current APIs such as `LaurentPolynomial.T n` and local notation `K[T;T⁻¹]`; do not use bare polynomial `X` for Laurent polynomials.",
        "Do not make typeclass objects (`CommRing α`, `Algebra R A`, etc.) components of an `∧`; those are types/classes, not propositions. Put them as assumptions/instances or use theorem statements about terms instead.",
        "If the source theorem is a narrow identity, formalize that identity directly rather than a broad bundled theorem whose components will not match the grader's withheld statement."
      ],
      "available_reference_corpus": [
        "Current-version mathlib source can be searched locally at /tmp/mathlib4-v4.28.0-src when preparing/evaluating prompts.",
        "Use nearby repository Lean prefix/local examples first; use mathlib source snippets only as API/style evidence, not as a source for the withheld target statement."
      ]
    },
    "local_lean_style": {
      "guidance": [
        "Match the local Lean style shown in local_lean_style.examples when it applies; prefer exact APIs and argument order already used in this file.",
        "Use only identifiers that appear in the Lean prefix context/local examples or are standard Mathlib identifiers; do not cite or invent raw helper names from guessed notation expansions.",
        "If the local examples use a domain-specific API such as `Ring.choose`, `updateCol`, or a local theorem name, keep that exact API family instead of falling back to a more familiar but different API such as `Nat.choose` or `Matrix.updateColumn`."
      ],
      "notation_contracts": [],
      "examples": [
        "/-- The two alternant definitions are equal.\n    This uses `AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det` to bridge the sum-based\n    alternant definition with the determinant-based alternantTextbook. -/\ntheorem alternantTextbook_eq_alternant (α : Fin N → ℕ) :\n    alternantTextbook (R := R) N α = alternant N α := by\n  unfold alternantTextbook alternant\n  rw [AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternant_eq_det]\n  unfold AlgebraicCombinatorics.alternantMatrix\n  rw [← Matrix.det_transpose]\n  congr 1"
      ]
    },
    "domain_statement_shape_guidance": [],
    "mathlib_context": [
      "Mathlib APIs referenced by imported modules; exact proof-level facts not statically certified.",
      "Context-selection output; generated from source-only prompt before any gold comparison.",
      "Source focus: Lemma lem.sf.alternant-0(b): If β is obtained from α by swapping two entries, then a_β = -a_α.",
      "Selected source part: lem.sf.alternant-0 part (b)",
      "Source-part rationale: Part (a) is already formalized as `alternant_zero_of_eq` in the prefix context. The remaining part (b) should be formalized.",
      "Formalization sketch:",
      "- Or more directly: for a transposition swapping i and j, alternant (α ∘ swap i j) = -alternant α",
      "- Proof sketch: Use the definition of alternant as ∑_{τ} sign(τ) · x^(α∘τ). Then substitute τ' = τ∘σ, use sign(τ∘σ) = sign(τ)·sign(σ) and sign(σ) = -1 for a swap.",
      "Selected candidate Mathlib/API context:",
      "- `Equiv.Perm.sign_swap`; shape: sign (swap i j) = -1 if i ≠ j; why: The sign of a transposition is -1, used to get the negation.; confidence: 0.95",
      "- `Equiv.Perm.sign_mul`; shape: sign (σ * τ) = sign σ * sign τ; why: To factor sign(τ∘σ) = sign(τ)·sign(σ) in the proof.; confidence: 0.95",
      "- `Equiv.swap_comp_eq`; shape: swap i j ∘ σ = ...; why: May be needed to rewrite composition with a swap.; confidence: 0.6",
      "Proof notes:",
      "- Let σ be the transposition swapping the two entries. Then alternant (α ∘ σ) = ∑_τ sign(τ) · x^(α∘σ∘τ) = ∑_τ sign(τ) · x^(α∘(σ∘τ)).",
      "- Reindex by τ' = σ∘τ, so τ = σ∘τ' (since σ is its own inverse). Then sign(τ) = sign(σ)·sign(τ') = -sign(τ').",
      "- Thus alternant (α ∘ σ) = ∑_{τ'} (-sign(τ')) · x^(α∘τ') = -alternant α.",
      "Uncertainties to resolve during repair/review:",
      "- Whether the alternant definition uses `α ∘ σ` or `σ • α` (permutation action on the tuple). The prefix shows `α ∘ σ` in the definition.",
      "- Whether the lemma should be stated for a general swap or for any permutation that swaps two entries (i.e., a transposition). The source says 'swapping two entries', which suggests a transposition.",
      "Hydrated Mathlib source snippets:",
      "Mathlib source match for `Equiv.Perm.sign_swap` at Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/Sign.lean:388:\n```lean\ntheorem sign_swap {x y : α} (h : x ≠ y) : sign (swap x y) = -1 :=\n  (signAux3_mul_and_swap 1 1 _ mem_univ).2 h\n\n@[simp]\n```",
      "Mathlib source match for `Equiv.Perm.sign_swap` at Mathlib/GroupTheory/Perm/Sign.lean:392:\n```lean\ntheorem sign_swap' {x y : α} : sign (swap x y) = if x = y then 1 else -1 :=\n  if H : x = y then by simp [H, swap_self] else by simp [sign_swap H, H]\n```",
      "Mathlib source match for `Equiv.Perm.sign_mul` at Mathlib/Data/EReal/Inv.lean:103:\n```lean\ntheorem sign_mul (x y : EReal) : sign (x * y) = sign x * sign y := by\n  induction x, y using induction₂_symm_neg with\n  | top_zero => simp only [mul_zero, sign_zero]\n  | top_top => rfl\n  | symm h => rwa [mul_comm, EReal.mul_comm]\n  | coe_coe => simp only [← coe_mul, sign_coe, _root_.sign_mul]\n  | top_pos _ h =>\n    rw [top_mul_coe_of_pos h, sign_top, one_mul, sign_pos (EReal.coe_pos.2 h)]\n  | neg_left h => rw [neg_mul, sign_neg, sign_neg, h, neg_mul]\n\n@[simp] protected theorem sign_mul_abs : ∀ x : EReal, (sign x * x.abs : EReal) = x\n  | ⊥ => by simp\n  | ⊤ => by simp\n  | (x : ℝ) => by rw [sign_coe, coe_abs, ← coe_coe_sign, ← coe_mul, sign_mul_abs]\n\n@[simp] protected theorem abs_mul_sign (x : EReal) : (x.abs * sign x : EReal) = x := by\n  rw [EReal.mul_comm, EReal.sign_mul_abs]\n```",
      "Mathlib source match for `Equiv.Perm.sign_mul` at Mathlib/Data/Sign/Basic.lean:100:\n```lean\ntheorem sign_mul (x y : α) : sign (x * y) = sign x * sign y := by\n  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with (hx | hx | hx) <;> rcases lt_trichotomy y 0 with (hy | hy | hy) <;>\n    simp [hx, hy, mul_pos_of_neg_of_neg, mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos, mul_neg_of_pos_of_neg]\n\n@[simp] theorem sign_mul_abs (x : α) : (sign x * |x| : α) = x := by\n  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with hx | rfl | hx <;> simp [*, abs_of_pos, abs_of_neg]\n\n@[simp] theorem abs_mul_sign (x : α) : (|x| * sign x : α) = x := by\n  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with hx | rfl | hx <;> simp [*, abs_of_pos, abs_of_neg]\n\n@[simp]\n```",
      "Mathlib query `Equiv.swap_comp_eq` returned no local source match."
    ],
    "benchmark_policy": {
      "target_lean_statement_available_to_model": false,
      "target_declaration_name_available_to_model": false,
      "grading_uses_withheld_gold_statement": true,
      "source_only_removed_hidden_target_context_blocks": 2
    }
  }
